//: TRANSFORM P8 — the Lighthouse release gate.
Every floor is a RATCHET set just under what the build actually measures
(mobile, simulated throttling, 3 runs, median), NOT an aspiration: a gate
that fails on every green build gets switched off, and a gate that nothing
can trip is not a gate.
Measured on the P8 export, sign-in shell: perf 50-53, a11y 88,
LCP and TBT are the KNOWN weakness — one ~2.5MB JS bundle, because Expo
web has no route-level code splitting on this pipeline. They are asserted
as warnings so the number stays visible in CI without blocking; the real
fix is native builds / splitting, tracked in EVOFORGE_TRANSFORM.md.
Raise a floor whenever the build clears it comfortably — never lower one
to make a red run green.

2026-07-18: URL changed /index.html -> / — expo-router treats the literal '/index.html' path as an UNMATCHED ROUTE, so the gate was auditing the 404 page (its image ratio, tiny text and hydration error), not the sign-in shell users load.

2026-07-19 (audit batch): measured on the post-leaning export — perf 49-51, FCP ~0.79-0.86s, LCP ~7.2-7.7s, TBT 1.17-1.38s (local 3-run + CI's 7232ms LCP). Ratcheted FCP 2000->1500 and TBT warn 1800->1700; perf/a11y floors unchanged — no fresh CI category measurements to justify raising them (CI prints only failed assertions, and the reports artifact upload has a path bug fixable only with workflow scope). LCP still warns — the ~2.6MB __common is the fix, tracked as route-level splitting.
